comment: Validation question banks for the score-only deny gate. IN_CORPUS: natural questions that SHOULD hit a seeded concept (a deny is a TRUE deny). ADVERSARIAL: questions the corpus does NOT confidently answer (a deny is a FALSE deny). Each adversarial question is tagged near|far. NEAR-MISS = topically adjacent to a seeded node (a different facet of the same domain that no node actually covers), engineered so its nearest-hit distance lands close to the hook's 1.05 cap. FAR-MISS = an unrelated domain whose nearest hit should sit well past the cap. The near/far split is the whole point: it probes whether the distance filter actually holds when adversarial queries are semantically close rather than random.
in_corpus: how does Raft pick a new leader when the old one dies?
what makes a Raft log entry count as committed?
how does Paxos guarantee a chosen value can't be unchosen?
why can two-phase commit get stuck holding locks?

how do CRDTs converge without any coordination between replicas?
how does gossip spread updates across a big cluster?

what does the CAP theorem actually force you to choose between?
how do R and W quorums give you tunable consistency?
how does HNSW search for nearest neighbors so fast?
how does product quantization shrink vectors in memory?
when should I use cosine similarity instead of Euclidean distance?

adversarial.q: how does Raft handle reconfiguring cluster membership while it's running?
what is the message complexity of a single Paxos round in the worst case?

how does the SPLADE model produce learned sparse lexical weights?
how does the ColBERT late-interaction scoring differ from a cross-encoder?
how does a skip list support ordered range queries with probabilistic balancing?
how does the Bellman-Ford algorithm handle negative edge weights for shortest paths?
how does a counting Bloom filter support deletions that a standard one can't?
how does a fractal tree index reduce write amplification compared to an LSM tree?
how does ARIES use redo and undo passes during crash recovery?
how does serializable snapshot isolation detect and abort write-skew anomalies?
how does an admission policy like TinyLFU outperform plain LRU on scan-heavy loads?
how does the leaky bucket algorithm smooth output rate differently from a token bucket?
